title: utania volubilis
intro: Utania volubilis, commonly known as the "Wild Jasmine" or "Indian Jasmine", is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to promote relaxation, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of insomnia and digestive issues. The health benefits of Utania volubilis include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its therapeutic actions of sedation, anxiolysis, and gastrointestinal relief. The bioactive constituents of Utania volubilis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and have been studied for their potential in treating various health conditions.
intro_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Urtania volubilis (also known as Uncaria tomentosa) benefits include improved repair of DNA single strand breaks and double strand breaks after radiation exposure.
side_effects: Utania volubilis can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing dizziness, nausea, and headaches in individuals who consume it in excess. In some cases, it may also lead to allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Prolonged use or misuse of the plant can result in liver and kidney damage, as well as interactions with other medications. Additionally, it can cause digestive issues like stomach cramps, diarrhea, and vomiting, especially when taken without proper guidance or in combination with other substances.
